description: Convert Ctrl+Shift+/ from cycling through all projects to a 'boss key' that only switches to the default (first) project instantly. Default project must be password-free. All other projects require passwords via Ctrl+Shift+P panel.
steps: Step 1 - Modify Ctrl+Shift+/ handler: Instead of cycling to next project, find the default/first project and switch to it immediately via cycleToProject. If already on the default project, do nothing.

Step 2 - Make the default project (first project, index 0) password-free: Remove password from the default project, ensure it never requires authentication. On init, set passwordEnabled to false if the active project is the default.

Step 3 - Require password for all non-default projects in the panel switch mode: When not on the lock gate, clicking a non-default project in the Switch list should ask for the password before switching. Only the default project can be switched to without password.
Step 4 - Disable password setting for the default project: Hide or disable the 'Change Key' option when on the default project.
Step 5 - Add subtle default indicator in the secret panel: Show a small indicator next to the default project name in the panel lists.
acceptance_criteria: Ctrl+Shift+/ only switches to the default (first) project, not cycling through all
If already on the default project, Ctrl+Shift+/ does nothing
The default project never has a password and never shows the lock gate
Switching to non-default projects from the panel requires entering the password
The default project can be switched to from the panel without a password
Change Key option is hidden when on the default project
